result.summary: Found 4 visual issues on the page, ranging from major layout clipping problems to minor truncation issues. The most notable problems involve game cards and navigation elements being cut off on the right side of the screen.

issues.description: Right-column game cards are visually clipped/cropped, cutting off game titles mid-word (e.g., 'MAGNI PHO...', 'MUNC MI...', 'HYPERO PYRAMIO...', 'DAR WATE THE CURS...'). The partial images and unreadable titles degrade the user experience significantly.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the game card grid uses proper overflow handling or responsive columns so cards are either fully visible or clearly indicate they are scrollable/swipeable. Consider reducing card size or adjusting the grid layout to show complete content.

issues.description: The top navigation tab bar clips the last tab label, showing 'Hot Ga...' instead of the full label (likely 'Hot Games'). This makes the navigation option unrecognizable and harder to tap accurately.
issues.suggestion: Implement a horizontally scrollable tab bar with full labels visible, or use a scrollable chip row so users can see and access all navigation categories without text truncation.

issues.description: The promotional banner text 'BET IT ON BLACK AND GOLD!' references Pittsburgh Penguins branding but the visual context (casino games on phones) doesn't strongly reinforce the sports tie-in, making the message feel disconnected.
issues.suggestion: Add clearer Penguins branding or a sports-betting CTA to align the banner message with its visual content for better clarity and cohesion.

issues.description: The bottom navigation bar 'Casino' item appears underlined and uses a different icon style compared to other nav items, which may cause visual inconsistency in the active state styling.
issues.suggestion: Standardize the active state styling across all bottom navigation items using a consistent indicator such as a colored underline or highlighted icon/label for all tabs.
